text: All the students of UG/PG programs are expected to attend every lecture, tutorial, practical or design studio class scheduled for them. The students of UG/PG program must have a minimum attendance of 75% of the total number of classes including lectures, tutorials and practicals, held in a subject in order to be eligible to appear at the End-Term Examination for that subject. The Dean (UG)/Dean (PG), authorized by the Vice Chancellor for this purpose may relax the minimum attendance upto 10% for reasons to be recorded. This relaxation may be granted on production of documents showing that the student was either busy in the authorized activities or suffering from any disease. The student should submit these documents to the Dean (UG)/Dean (PG) through Chairman, BoS within seven days of resuming the studies. Under exceptional circumstances, the Vice Chancellor may further relax the minimum attendance upto 5%.
section_heading: R. 1(B).22 Attendance, Absence, Leave and Withdrawals

text: The 'F' grade denotes poor performance, i.e. failing a course. 'F' grade is also awarded in case of poor attendance. 'F' grade secured in any course stays permanently on the grade card. The weight of 'F' grade is not counted in the calculation of the CGPA however, it is counted in the calculation of the SGPA. In case a student is awarded 'F' Grade in a Core paper, the student has to repeat the course either in the summer semester or in subsequent semester, when it is offered. The students having 'F' grade in a subject in either or both odd and even semesters, at the end of current Academic Year shall be allowed to register for summer semester. In case a student is awarded 'F' grade in an Elective paper, the student may take the same course or any other course from the same category in the subsequent semester.
section_heading: R. 1(B).17 Grading System - Award of F Grade

text: The enrolment of a student in a program shall stand terminated if he/she fails to earn 30% of prescribed credits in first year, as specified in the Scheme of teaching and examinations, at the end of first year. The enrolment of a student shall stand cancelled on completion of maximum duration from the date of initial registration in the program. The normal duration of B.Tech is 4 years and the maximum duration is 7 years. A student whose enrolment has been terminated may appeal to the Vice Chancellor for reconsideration within fifteen days from the date of issuance of the communication of termination. If the appeal is allowed, his/her registration and enrolment shall be restored.
section_heading: R. 1(B).25 Termination of Enrolment on Academic Grounds

text: Student should first register for the courses in which he/she has been declared failed in the previous year/semester and then register for the remaining courses of the semester to make up the total required credits for that semester. A student of a program of more than 2 years duration shall not be allowed to register for the courses offered to students of third year, if he/she has not cleared all the courses of first year. A student of a program of more than 3 years duration shall not be allowed to register for the courses offered to students of fourth year, if he/she has not cleared all the courses of second year. For B.Tech, a student can register for a minimum of 16 credits and a maximum of 32 credits per semester.
section_heading: R. 1(B).11 Course Registration and Backlog Rules

text: A student will be allowed to register a maximum of 16 credits in the summer semester, inclusive of odd and even semester courses. Only common courses (First year), departmental core courses (DCC) and allied engineering courses (AEC) will be offered in summer semester. The duration of the summer semester will be 45 days. Regular classroom teaching will be conducted only for those courses where minimum number of registered students is 5% of approved intake or 15 whichever is less. Registration cum examination fee will be Rs. 5000/- + Rs. 500/- per course. Students will be allowed to register for courses only after depositing the requisite fee. Registration in summer semester course(s) will be offered only w.e.f AY 2020-21 batches.
section_heading: Annexure-3 Guidelines for Summer Semester Courses

text: The academic performance of a student shall be graded on a 10-point scale. A student to get passing grade must appear in End-Term Examination. The passing marks is 35% (grade P with grade point 4). A student with CGPA of 8.0 and above shall be awarded 'First Division with Distinction' and a student with CGPA between 6.0 and 8.0 shall be awarded 'First Division'. The CGPA is calculated as the weighted average of grade points of all courses in which the student obtained grade P or above. The weight of 'F' grade is not counted in the calculation of CGPA. All passing out students of a class shall be given ranks as 'Rank XXX in a class of YYY Students'.
section_heading: R. 1(B).17 Grading System and CGPA Calculation

text: The letter grades and their grade points at DTU are as follows: O (Outstanding) = 10 points, awarded when marks >= mean + 1.5*standard deviation or 91% whichever is lower; A+ (Excellent) = 9 points; A (Very Good) = 8 points; B+ (Good) = 7 points; B (Above average) = 6 points; C (Average) = 5 points; P (Pass) = 4 points, awarded for marks between 35% and mean - 1.0*standard deviation; F (Fail) = 0 points, awarded for marks below 35%. In those subjects where the total number of students is up to 30, results in Grades shall be prepared on the basis of actual performance in percentage scale and not on the basis of the formula.
section_heading: R. 1(B).17 Grading System - Grade Structure Table

text: A student may apply for scrutiny of grades to the Head of the Department/School, within three days from the date of display of grades. The Grade Moderation Committee and the Course Coordinator may check the entry of the weights from different components of evaluation and their addition. The results of scrutiny may lead to either a change in grade due to mistake(s) in any of the aspects scrutinized by the committee or the grade may remain unchanged. The results will be intimated to the COE within three days from the date of receiving the application in the department/school as per academic calendar. The answer sheets of the End-Term Examination shall not be shown to a student after finalization of the grades by the Grade Moderation Committee.

Students who have missed the Mid-Term Examinations and/or End-Term Examinations of a course due to medical reasons or other special circumstances may be considered for a Make-up Examination on the clear cut recommendations of HOD considering the merit of the case. In case of End-Term Examinations, Make-up Examinations will be allowed only if a student has not been disqualified earlier due to shortage of attendance. The student shall make an application to the HOD, within ten working days from the date of the examination missed, explaining the reasons for his/her absence. Applications received after this period will not be entertained. There will be no make-up of the Make-up Examinations.
section_heading: R. 1(B).23 Make-up Examinations on Medical/Extra Ordinary Ground

text: Every student shall be required to register in each semester on the scheduled date as per academic calendar of the University till the completion of the degree. If the student does not register on scheduled date he/she has to pay late registration fee notified from time to time upto a maximum of 10 working days. Registration in absentia may be permitted by the Dean (UG)/Dean (PG). In absentia registration may be allowed only in rare cases such as illness or any other contingencies, at the discretion of the Dean (UG)/Dean (PG). A student shall have the option to add or delete courses from his/her registration during the first ten days of the semester as per Academic Calendar.

If a student is absent during End-Term Examination of a course due to medical reasons or other special circumstances, he/she may apply for the award of 'I' (Incomplete) grade to the COE through the Course Coordinator, provided that he/she has not been disqualified due to shortage of attendance. The 'I' grade shall be converted into a proper letter grade after make-up examination is over. In extraordinary circumstances, the period of conversion of 'I' grade may be extended to next semester, with the approval of the Dean (UG)/Dean (PG). There will be no make-up of the Make-up Examinations.

A student who wants to withdraw from a course shall apply through the HOD to the COE within one week from the end of the Mid-Term Examination under the advice of his/her Program Advisor. If his/her request for withdrawal is granted, it will be recorded in the registration record of the student and the concerned Course Coordinator will be informed. In case a student is unable to attend classes for more than four weeks in a semester he/she may apply to the COE through HOD for withdrawal from the semester, which shall mean withdrawal from all the registered courses in the semester. Such application shall be made latest before the start of the End-Term Examination. Partial withdrawal from the semester shall not be allowed.
section_heading: R. 1(B).24 Withdrawal from Course and Semester

text: DTU has 11 boys and 4 girls hostels in the campus to accommodate around 1800 boys and 800 girls. Hostel accommodation will be allotted to full-time students by the University Hostel Allotment Committee depending upon availability of seats in the hostels. Application for hostel allotment should be submitted in the prescribed form within stipulated time by the students. No ex-students shall be allotted any hostel accommodation. The first preference for hostel accommodation for the 1st semester of B.Tech students will be given to the outside Delhi Category and so it may be possible that Delhi Category candidates may not get hostel accommodation. Hostel allotment will be provided on the basis of the category selected by the candidate during admission at DTU. No further changes of category will be allowed.

Hostel fee per semester at DTU: For Non-AC Hostels: Single Seater - Room Rent Rs.22,000 + Electricity & Water Rs.2,000 + Security & Attendant Rs.2,000 + Medical Fee Rs.1,000 + Hostel Maintenance Rs.3,000 = Grand Total Rs.30,000 per semester. Double Seater - Grand Total Rs.28,000 per semester. Triple Seater - Grand Total Rs.26,000 per semester. For AC Hostels (Triple Seater): Room Rent Rs.18,000 + Electricity & Water Rs.12,000 + Security Rs.2,000 + Medical Rs.1,000 + Maintenance Rs.3,000 = Grand Total Rs.36,000 per semester. Hostel rent is payable in advance, before possession of the room. Fee has to be paid only after confirmation of allotment.

All hostel residents are required to vacate their rooms within a week of the last examination each year. The rooms will be re-allocated at the beginning of the academic session. On special occasions, if a student wants to stay in a hostel with some other students not exceeding 5 days, he/she shall pay guest charges Rs.300/- per day for Non-AC and Rs.500/- per day for AC hostels respectively. For all enquiries regarding hostel accommodation, students should contact the hostel office in Transit Hostel (Timing: 10:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m.) during working days. Students may apply for fresh hostel allotment online via hostel website: hostels.dtu.ac.in.

The University shall award the merit-cum-means (MCM) scholarships, University free studentship, SC/ST category University scholarship and other scholarships to students of UG programs. Merit scholarship is awarded strictly on principle of academic merit to the toppers of all branches of B.Tech program annually, with the condition that the student concerned has secured 75% or above as aggregate marks or CGPA of 7.5 or above in an academic year. The student must have passed all subjects of the semester in one attempt. The student should have a sound moral character and should not have indulged into any act of misconduct. In case of tie, all awardees will be given Rs 5,000/- each.

Those students who have been punished for unfair means during MTE or ETE or in Seminars/project or for serious act of indiscipline shall not be awarded Merit-cum-Means Scholarship and other trust scholarships or Medals, Prizes and awards for that academic session only. Students may draw scholarships from outside sources only if permitted by Dean (UG)/Dean (PG). Announcements on scholarships stating eligibility and the value of scholarships shall be made by the University while inviting applications from time to time.

DTU has well established guidelines for extending fee concession to needy students with lean economical background. Students whose family income from all sources is less than Rs. 4,50,000/- per annum will only be eligible for fee concession. Income Certificate should be valid at the time of online application, issued by SDM/1st Class Magistrate. Applications are submitted online on www.btechstudentportal.dtu.ac.in (portal is active in the month of August/September). All shortlisted students will be required to appear for personal interview by a designated Committee. The full fee concession may be given to a maximum of 5% of the sanctioned intake for the particular year in the respective program.
section_heading: Fee Concession for Economically Weaker Sections

text: For the Semester Away Program at DTU, UG students of 4-year duration must complete 4 semesters at DTU, have a CGPA of 7.0 or above at the time of application without any backlog, and have no disciplinary action initiated. Students shall be allowed to register equivalent credits ranging from 8 to 20 at the host institution. All core courses should be completed within their prescribed schedule at DTU. DTU shall not provide any financial support for the Program. Application deadline is 31st July for Even Semester and 31st January for Odd Semester. Applications are submitted to the office of Dean International Affairs (IA).

In case a student is found adopting or suspected of adopting unfair means before, during or after the examination, or lifting or copying of work of someone else without proper acknowledgement, such penal action shall be taken by the University against the student as may be necessary. All cases regarding use of unfair means shall be reported and placed before the Unfair Means Scrutiny Committee. The Committee is chaired by Dean (UG) and includes Dean (PG), DSW, Head of the concerned Department, Associate Dean Academic (UG), Associate Dean Academic (PG) as members, and COE as Convener. A fresh answer-book shall be given to the student for completing the examination after the original is seized.

The Mid-Term Examination will be conducted by course coordinator and the faculty members concerned during the slot notified by the COE. The End-Term Examination will be conducted by the COE. Evaluation process should be concluded within specified days: Mid-Term Examination within one week, End-Term Examination within two weeks from the end of the schedule of examination. A student can see his/her answer sheet of MTE and ETE and point out any discrepancy in its evaluation on a day fixed by the Course Coordinator. Objections will be entertained right then, and not even on the next day. Answer sheets be disposed off by the Examination Branch after preserving them for six months.

The University offers 14 undergraduate engineering programs and 25 post graduate engineering programs. The B.Tech program has a normal duration of 4 years and maximum duration of 7 years. The programs are based on Choice Based Credit System (CBCS). The university is accredited with 'A' grade by NAAC and has been accorded 12-B status by UGC. Several UG engineering programs are also accredited by the National Board of Accreditation (NBA). The 2021 NIRF rankings placed DTU at the 36th position among engineering institutions and 42nd in the category of universities.

DTU campus is 164 acres with 150,000 sq. mtr. of built up area, 14 academic departments, and residences for students, faculty and staff. The university has around 12,000 students in undergraduate, postgraduate, and PhD programmes. DTU has a full-fledged health centre where services of five medical practitioners and round the clock nursing facilities are available. The health centre has general physicians Monday to Saturday, an eye specialist, dentist, orthopaedician, gynaecologist, and psychiatrist. DTU has a wireless network connecting the computer centre, academic wings, administrative block, and hostels on a common platform with high speed internet.

A student shall have the option to obtain a Minor in a specific discipline. A Minor shall be awarded to a student along with his/her major discipline if he/she has earned requisite credits from the prescribed basket of Courses for that Minor. A student who wishes to pursue a Minor from another discipline shall earn 24 credits from the basket prescribed for that Minor out of the total 48 credits to be earned from elective courses. A student who wishes to pursue a Minor from the own discipline shall earn 20 credits from the basket prescribed for a particular Minor. An elective course in the department shall run if a minimum of 20 students register for it in a semester.

The rules for change of name in academic records at DTU are divided into two categories. Category A (Correction): Correction in name to correct spelling errors, typographical errors may be considered within 5 years of the declaration of results. Category B (Change): Change in name/surname of candidate, date of birth, name of father/mother/guardian may be considered within 5 years of declaration of results provided the changes have been admitted by CBSE/State Boards. Required documents include: application in prescribed format forwarded by Head of Department, affidavit on non-judicial stamp paper of Rs. 20/-, self-attested copy of Roll Number issued by the University, and fee receipt of prescribed fee. In case mistake is from University side, no fee will be charged.
section_heading: Rules for Change of Name of Student in Academic Records

text: The promotion criteria at DTU: A student of B.Tech program shall not be allowed to register for courses of third year if he/she has not cleared all courses of first year. Similarly, a student shall not be allowed to register for courses of fourth year if he/she has not cleared all courses of second year. The enrolment of a student shall stand terminated if he/she fails to earn 30% of prescribed credits in first year at the end of first year. The maximum duration of B.Tech is 7 years from the date of initial registration. Students must complete all degree requirements within this maximum duration.
section_heading: Promotion Criteria from Year to Year

text: DTU provides financial assistance to students for presenting Research Paper/Poster in National/International Conference/Events. This scheme promotes research and development activities by providing opportunity to students to interact at national and international level. There is also financial assistance for students for attending internship overseas, instituted keeping in view the importance of state of the art research and international exposure. Financial Assistance for Students' Innovative Projects is available to strengthen student innovative projects and facilitate student teams venturing for innovative projects. Approval for these schemes was accorded during the 19th meeting of Academic Council held on 16.11.2018.

The evaluation of courses at DTU includes: Class Work Sessional (CWS), Mid-Term Examination (MTE), End-Term Examination (ETE), and Practical Examination (PRE). For a 3-credit theory course (3L 0T 0P): CWS = 15 marks, MTE = 25 marks weighting, ETE = 40 marks weighting. For practical courses: PRS/STS = 50 marks and PRE = 50 marks. The criteria for continuous evaluation of any subject shall be declared in the very first week of commencement of the classes. Answer sheets of tests and examinations cannot be written in pencil. Evaluation of answer sheets should not be in pencil.

Fee concession for hostel at DTU: Students whose family income from all sources is less than Rs. 4,50,000/- per annum are eligible to apply for fee concession. Students of 1st year and 3rd year shall deposit the University annual fee at the start of the academic session and fee concession amount will be reimbursed if the student fulfils the criteria. In 2nd and 4th year the student will deposit the fee as per the applicable fee concession received in 1st and 3rd year respectively. Any student who availed fee concession in 1st year will continue to be eligible for Fee Concession in 2nd year also, provided he/she has acquired minimum required credits for promotion.

The course code system at DTU: Each course offered by the University is identified by a course code consisting of a string of five alpha-numeric characters followed by a course title. The first two characters are capital letters identifying the responsible Academic Department/School. The next three characters are numerical digits: the first one normally specifies the year of study and the last two digits specify the course number and the semester in which the course shall be offered. Normally odd number in the course code indicates the course will be offered in the odd-semester and the even number indicates the even-semester. One lecture hour per week is normally assigned one credit. One laboratory or Design Studio hour per week is normally assigned half a credit.
section_heading: R. 1(B).8 and R. 1(B).9 Course Code and Credits System
